title: Holy Trinity Church, Powai
tagline: Holy Trinity Church, Powai — spiritual town of Maharashtra

overview.short: Holy Trinity Church or the Most Holy Trinity is a Roman Catholic church in Powai, a suburb of Mumbai.
overview.description: Holy Trinity Church or the Most Holy Trinity is a Roman Catholic church in Powai, a suburb of Mumbai. It was built during the Portuguese era by the Jesuits in 1557 and belongs to the Archdiocese of Bombay.

topPlaces.name: Kendriya Vidyalaya, IIT Powai
topPlaces.description: Kendriya Vidyalaya, IIT Powai (also known as Kendriya Vidyalaya, IIT Bombay and KV Powai), is an Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) secondary school affiliated to Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) board in Mumbai, India. It was established on 15 December 1964.

topPlaces.name: Mini Sabarimala
topPlaces.description: Mini Sabarimala, situated on a hillock surrounded by hills and valleys within the precincts of NCH Colony, Kanjurmarg (West), Mumbai, is the first ancient Ayyappa Temple outside Kerala State. As revealed by the astrologer Shri Karuvatta Kochugovindan, there was a big Devi temple and a small Ayyappa temple at this place, where today stands the temple of Shree Mini Sabarimala.

topPlaces.name: IIT Bombay
topPlaces.description: The Indian Institute of Technology Bombay (IIT Bombay or IITB) is a public research university and technical institute in Mumbai, Maharashtra, India. The institute has 17 academic departments, 35 additional academic centres, and three schools.

topPlaces.name: Techfest
topPlaces.description: Techfest, Asia's largest science and technology festival, is the annual science and technology festival of the Indian Institute of Technology Bombay, consisting of social initiatives and outreach programs throughout the year. Started in 1998 with the aim of providing a platform for the Indian student community to develop and showcase their projects, with a footfall of 1.8 lakhs in its latest edition.

topPlaces.name: Central Library, IIT Bombay
topPlaces.description: The Library is one of the central support services of IIT Bombay. The mission of the Library is to provide information services and access to bibliographic and full text digital and printed resources in order to support the scholarly and information needs of the students, faculty and staff at the Institute Community.

topPlaces.name: IIT Bombay Racing
topPlaces.description: IIT Bombay Racing is a Formula Student team from India based at Indian Institute of Technology Bombay since 2007. IIT Bombay Racing made its debut at Formula Student Michigan, 2008 with an entry car Vayu as only Indian team participating in the event.

topPlaces.name: Centre for Excellence in Telecom Technology and Management
topPlaces.description: Centre for Excellence in Telecom Technology and Management (CETTM) located in Hiranandani Gardens, Powai, Mumbai is the telecom training centre of MTNL, the incumbent operator of Government of India (GOI). It was founded in 2003 as one of the largest telecom training centres in India and amongst the biggest in Asia.

image.alt: Centre for Excellence in Telecom Technology and Management

topPlaces.name: Bhandup
topPlaces.description: Bhandup (Pronunciation: [bʱaːɳɖup]) () is a suburban locality in Mumbai, in the state of Maharashtra, India. The name Bhandup is derived from Bhandupeshwar, one of the names of the Hindu god Shiva.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Kendriya Vidyalaya, IIT Powai & around

items.note: Kendriya Vidyalaya, IIT Powai (also known as Kendriya Vidyalaya, IIT Bombay and KV Powai), is an Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) secondary school affiliated to Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) board in Mumbai, India. It was established on 15 December 1964.

Mini Sabarimala, situated on a hillock surrounded by hills and valleys within the precincts of NCH Colony, Kanjurmarg (West), Mumbai, is the first ancient Ayyappa Temple outside Kerala State. As revealed by the astrologer Shri Karuvatta Kochugovindan, there was a big Devi temple and a small Ayyappa temple at this place, where today stands the temple of Shree Mini Sabarimala.

The Indian Institute of Technology Bombay (IIT Bombay or IITB) is a public research university and technical institute in Mumbai, Maharashtra, India. The institute has 17 academic departments, 35 additional academic centres, and three schools.

Techfest, Asia's largest science and technology festival, is the annual science and technology festival of the Indian Institute of Technology Bombay, consisting of social initiatives and outreach programs throughout the year. Started in 1998 with the aim of providing a platform for the Indian student community to develop and showcase their projects, with a footfall of 1.8 lakhs in its latest edition.

The Library is one of the central support services of IIT Bombay. The mission of the Library is to provide information services and access to bibliographic and full text digital and printed resources in order to support the scholarly and information needs of the students, faculty and staff at the Institute Community.

IIT Bombay Racing is a Formula Student team from India based at Indian Institute of Technology Bombay since 2007. IIT Bombay Racing made its debut at Formula Student Michigan, 2008 with an entry car Vayu as only Indian team participating in the event.

It was founded in 2003 as one of the largest telecom training centres in India and amongst the biggest in Asia.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Holy Trinity Church, Powai?
faq.a: The best time to visit Holy Trinity Church, Powai is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Holy Trinity Church, Powai?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (7 km) and the nearest railway station is Mumbai CSMT (23 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Holy Trinity Church, Powai?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Holy Trinity Church, Powai at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Holy Trinity Church, Powai?
faq.a: Stays in Holy Trinity Church, Powai start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Holy Trinity Church, Powai?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kendriya Vidyalaya, IIT Powai, Mini Sabarimala, IIT Bombay, Techfest, Central Library, IIT Bombay.
